reasoning: sygiznïŋ piri = 1/8 (sygiz=8, -niŋ piri = 1/n), iki sygistig = 2/8 (iki=2, sygis=8, -tig = nth part), sygizniŋ ÿzi = 3/8 (ÿz=3)
čÿti ÿstig = 7/3 (čÿti=7, ÿs=3), altuunuŋ piri = 1/6 (altuu=6), result = 5/2 = 2½
toğuus=9, iki toğuustuuğ = 2/9, toğuuznuŋ piri = 1/9, iki altuuluğ = 2/6 = 1/3 (altuu=6)
čÿtiniŋ ikizi = 2/7 (čÿti=7, ikizi means 2/n), iki pÿstig = 2/5 (iki=2, pÿs=5). This equation helped identify čÿti=7
altuunuŋ ikizi = 2/6 (altuu=6), toğuuznuŋ piri = 1/9 (toğuuz=9), sygis on sygistig = 8/18 = 4/9 (on sygis = 18)
pir törttïg = 1/4 (pir=1, tört=4), ÿs čÿbirgilig = 3/20 (ÿs=3, čÿbirgil=20), pir onnuğ = 1/10 (on=10)
15 = on pÿs (10+5, following pattern seen in compound numbers)

pÿdin appears in mixed number constructions (pir pÿdin pir čÿtlig = 1 1/7, iki pÿdin pir ikilig = 2 1/2) and functions as a connector between the whole number part and the fractional part, similar to 'and' in English mixed number expressions
